class WalletOk_26:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

wallet = WalletOk_26(100)
other = WalletOk_26(10)

    a, b, c = sorted([a, b, c])

class WalletOk_56:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

class WalletOk_86:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

runtime_item.code:
class WalletOk_116:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

wallet = WalletOk_116(100)
other = WalletOk_116(10)
assert wallet.transfer(20, other) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80
assert other.balance == 30
assert wallet.transfer(10, wallet) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80

class WalletOk_146:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

class WalletOk_176:
    def __init__(self, balance):

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

runtime_item.statement: Implement `WalletOk_206.transfer` so normal transfers move funds and self-transfers keep balances unchanged.
runtime_item.code:
class WalletOk_206:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

wallet = WalletOk_206(100)
other = WalletOk_206(10)
assert wallet.transfer(20, other) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80
assert other.balance == 30
assert wallet.transfer(10, wallet) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80

runtime_item.code:
class WalletOk_236:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

wallet = WalletOk_236(100)
other = WalletOk_236(10)
assert wallet.transfer(20, other) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80
assert other.balance == 30
assert wallet.transfer(10, wallet) is True
assert wallet.balance == 80

class WalletOk_266:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

    a, b, c = sorted([a, b, c])

        return "equilateral"

class WalletOk_296:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

class WalletOk_326:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True

class WalletOk_356:
    def __init__(self, balance):
        self.balance = balance

    def transfer(self, amount, target):
        if amount > self.balance:
            return False
        if target is self:
            return True
        self.balance -= amount
        target.balance += amount
        return True
